sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 45-year-old male, presents with a 5-day history of right knee pain that began after a skiing accident on the weekend.
He reports that he twisted his knee upon landing from a jump and has experienced pain and swelling since then.
The patient denies hearing or feeling anything pop at the time of injury.
He has been able to get out of the water but experiences soreness with squatting or bending.
He has never had a similar injury before.
The patient is a frequent water skier and enjoys the sport.
He has a history of high blood pressure, which he manages with lisinopril 20mg daily, and reports checking his blood pressure at home, with readings of 125/80 mmHg.
He denies any side effects from the medication.
He has taken over-the-counter aleve for pain and applied a cold pack to the affected area.
The patient had his tonsils removed in the past.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of high blood pressure, for which he is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily.
He also had his tonsils removed.
He is a frequent water skier and enjoys outdoor activities.
Review of systems: The patient denies any numbness or tingling in his foot.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77 mmHg, which is within normal limits.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis and edema on the right knee, with an effusion present.
Palpation of the right medial knee is painful.
The patient has decreased range of motion, a negative varus and valgus test, a negative Lachman sign, and palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee, which is confirmed by the physical exam findings.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain right knee; Hypertension; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily to help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the right knee.
I ordered an X-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I advised the patient to rest the knee for a while and avoid further activity that may exacerbate the injury.
I continued the patient's current lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
I ordered a lipid panel to check for any potential effects of the lisinopril on lipid levels.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: I will be in touch with the patient after the results of the X-ray and lipid panel are available.
